behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: risk is in handoff between history-trimming logic and LLM request assembly, not just a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement is stated. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model judgment/output quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the chat service end-to-end in-process with a stub LLM transport that captures the outbound prompt payload. Provide >10 history messages and assert only the most recent 10 are sent, in correct order.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This requires module wiring (service -> prompt builder -> LLM client), which is above Unit but below System.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model output relevance/quality; stubbed responses would hide failure. Not primarily infra-contract validation. MULTI-STEP: this behavior is about one response-quality judgment, not a required multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative customer questions to the real model path and evaluate whether responses are on-topic and answer the asked intent (using rubric/human-or-LLM-as-judge with repeated trials).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly depends on real model behavior, which cannot be validated by deterministic fixtures.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: This behavior couples multiple agentic steps in sequence: initial generation, tone judgment, then regeneration trigger/path. LLM-DEP exists for tone identification. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic alone was considered, but rejected because behavior text explicitly requires both correct identification and regeneration sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real flow with casual-tier prompts likely to induce formal first drafts; verify the system flags formality, performs a second generation, and records that regeneration path occurred in sequence.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The description requires sequential agentic handoff, so Workflow (not just single-step Agentic) is needed.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: persistence correctness depends on module interaction (chat flow + persistence layer + schema mapping). REAL-INFRA triad for System is not explicit (no real HTTP/external API contract requested). Local DB round-trip readback is sufficient signal.
behaviors.test_description: Run chat flow against a real local test DB, trigger both single-response and regeneration cases, then query stored records to assert all events exist with correct ordering and timestamps.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Uses real module wiring plus local DB readback; no external system contract is required.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD #3 applies: must prove externally visible effect ('customer never sees' rejected draft) via round-trip at the service boundary. Integration-only in-process assertions can miss boundary leakage (e.g., API/streaming path). LLM output quality itself is not the core assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the deployed API/serving boundary in a regeneration-triggering scenario and assert the customer-visible response/stream contains only the final draft while internal logs still record the rejected draft as non-visible.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires external boundary observation (customer-visible output), which is System-level.
